item.text: I’ll inspect the CSV, compute the aggregates precisely, then create and mechanically validate both files.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "wc -l data/sales.csv && sed -n '1,999p' data/sales.csv && ruby -rcsv -rbigdecimal -rjson -e '
rows=CSV.read(\"data/sales.csv\",headers:true)
vals=rows.map{|r| BigDecimal(r[\"units\"])*BigDecimal(r[\"unit_price\"])}
products=Hash.new(0.to_d); regions=Hash.new(0.to_d)
rows.each_with_index{|r,i| products[r[\"product\"]]+=vals[i]; regions[r[\"region\"]]+=vals[i]}
s=vals.sort; med=s.length.odd? ? s[s.length/2] : (s[s.length/2-1]+s[s.length/2])/2
puts JSON.pretty_generate({total_revenue: vals.sum.to_f.round(2),orders: rows.length,median_order_value: med.to_f.round(2),top_product_by_revenue: products.max_by{|_,v|v}[0],revenue_by_region: regions.transform_values{|v|v.to_f.round(2)),product_totals: products.transform_values{|v|v.to_f.round(2)}})
'"

/bin/bash -lc "wc -l data/sales.csv && sed -n '1,999p' data/sales.csv && ruby -rcsv -rbigdecimal -rjson -e '
rows=CSV.read(\"data/sales.csv\",headers:true)
vals=rows.map{|r| BigDecimal(r[\"units\"])*BigDecimal(r[\"unit_price\"])}
products=Hash.new(0.to_d); regions=Hash.new(0.to_d)
rows.each_with_index{|r,i| products[r[\"product\"]]+=vals[i]; regions[r[\"region\"]]+=vals[i]}
s=vals.sort; med=s.length.odd? ? s[s.length/2] : (s[s.length/2-1]+s[s.length/2])/2
puts JSON.pretty_generate({total_revenue: vals.sum.to_f.round(2),orders: rows.length,median_order_value: med.to_f.round(2),top_product_by_revenue: products.max_by{|_,v|v}[0],revenue_by_region: regions.transform_values{|v|v.to_f.round(2)),product_totals: products.transform_values{|v|v.to_f.round(2)}})
'"

/bin/bash -lc "node -e 'const fs=require(\"fs\"); const ls=fs.readFileSync(\"data/sales.csv\",\"utf8\").trim().split(/\\r?\\n/); const h=ls.shift().split(\",\"); const rows=ls.map(x=>Object.fromEntries(x.split(\",\").map((v,i)=>[h[i],v]))); const cents=rows.map(r=>Number(r.units)*Math.round(Number(r.unit_price)*100)); const sum=x=>x.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); const p={},g={}; rows.forEach((r,i)=>{p[r.product]=(p[r.product]||0)+cents[i];g[r.region]=(g[r.region]||0)+cents[i]}); const s=[...cents].sort((a,b)=>a-b); console.log(JSON.stringify({total_cents:sum(cents),orders:rows.length,median_cents:(s[14]+s[15])/2,products:p,regions:g,sorted:s},null,2))'"

/bin/bash -lc "node -e 'const fs=require(\"fs\"); const ls=fs.readFileSync(\"data/sales.csv\",\"utf8\").trim().split(/\\r?\\n/); const h=ls.shift().split(\",\"); const rows=ls.map(x=>Object.fromEntries(x.split(\",\").map((v,i)=>[h[i],v]))); const cents=rows.map(r=>Number(r.units)*Math.round(Number(r.unit_price)*100)); const sum=x=>x.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0); const p={},g={}; rows.forEach((r,i)=>{p[r.product]=(p[r.product]||0)+cents[i];g[r.region]=(g[r.region]||0)+cents[i]}); const s=[...cents].sort((a,b)=>a-b); console.log(JSON.stringify({total_cents:sum(cents),orders:rows.length,median_cents:(s[14]+s[15])/2,products:p,regions:g,sorted:s},null,2))'"

item.text: The dataset has 30 orders totaling $7,154.44. I’ve also computed product, region, and median values in integer cents to avoid floating-point drift.
